prompt: Architecture decision. Context: team of 6, one part-time DevOps generalist, shipping in 4 months, modest initial traffic, one PostgreSQL database, likely future split between billing and core app, but not before first release. Choose the default architecture and justify it briefly.

Compress these persistent project rules into SIGIL memory capsules only:
- keep v2 client compatibility
- do not rename public auth endpoints
- prefer minimal diffs
- always test expiry boundaries
- always test cache invalidation boundaries

Compile this project brief into a SIGIL execution draft before any code changes.

Project rules:
- preserve v2 client compatibility
- prefer minimal diffs
- never rename public auth endpoints
- add boundary tests around expiry and cache invalidation
- avoid new dependencies unless strictly required
- verify with unit tests first, integration tests second
